50	export class Model {
51	  private type: DMMF.OutputType
52	  private createManyAndReturnType: undefined | DMMF.OutputType
53	  private updateManyAndReturnType: undefined | DMMF.OutputType
54	  private mapping?: DMMF.ModelMapping
55	  private dmmf: DMMFHelper
56	
57	  constructor(
58	    private readonly model: DMMF.Model,
59	    private readonly context: GenerateContext,
60	  ) {
61	    this.dmmf = context.dmmf
62	    this.type = this.context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name]
63	
64	    this.createManyAndReturnType = this.context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[getCreateManyAndReturnOutputType(model.name)]
65	    this.updateManyAndReturnType = this.context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[getUpdateManyAndReturnOutputType(model.name)]
66	    this.mapping = this.context.dmmf.mappings.modelOperations.find((m) => m.model === model.name)!
67	  }
68	
69	  private get argsTypes(): ts.Export<ts.TypeDeclaration>[] {
70	    const argsTypes: ts.Export<ts.TypeDeclaration>[] = []
71	    for (const action of Object.keys(DMMF.ModelAction)) {
72	      const fieldName = this.rootFieldNameForAction(action as DMMF.ModelAction)
73	      if (!fieldName) {
74	        continue
75	      }
76	      const field = this.dmmf.rootFieldMap[fieldName]
77	      if (!field) {
78	        throw new Error(`Oops this must not happen. Could not find field ${fieldName} on either Query or Mutation`)
79	      }
80	
81	      if (
82	        action === 'updateMany' ||
83	        action === 'deleteMany' ||
84	        action === 'createMany' ||
85	        action === 'findRaw' ||
86	        action === 'aggregateRaw'
87	      ) {
88	        argsTypes.push(
89	          new ArgsTypeBuilder(this.type, this.context, action as DMMF.ModelAction)
90	            .addSchemaArgs(field.args)
91	            .createExport(),
92	        )
93	      } else if (action === 'createManyAndReturn') {
94	        const args = new ArgsTypeBuilder(this.type, this.context, action as DMMF.ModelAction)
95	          .addSelectArg(getSelectCreateManyAndReturnName(this.type.name))
96	          .addOmitArg()
97	          .addSchemaArgs(field.args)
98	
99	        if (this.createManyAndReturnType) {
100	          args.addIncludeArgIfHasRelations(
101	            getIncludeCreateManyAndReturnName(this.model.name),
102	            this.createManyAndReturnType,
103	          )
104	        }
105	        argsTypes.push(args.createExport())
106	      } else if (action === 'updateManyAndReturn') {
107	        const args = new ArgsTypeBuilder(this.type, this.context, action as DMMF.ModelAction)
108	          .addSelectArg(getSelectUpdateManyAndReturnName(this.type.name))
109	          .addOmitArg()
110	          .addSchemaArgs(field.args)
111	
112	        if (this.updateManyAndReturnType) {
113	          args.addIncludeArgIfHasRelations(
114	            getIncludeUpdateManyAndReturnName(this.model.name),
115	            this.updateManyAndReturnType,
116	          )
117	        }
118	        argsTypes.push(args.createExport())
119	      } else if (action !== 'groupBy' && action !== 'aggregate') {
120	        argsTypes.push(
121	          new ArgsTypeBuilder(this.type, this.context, action as DMMF.ModelAction)
122	            .addSelectArg()
123	            .addOmitArg()
124	            .addIncludeArgIfHasRelations()
125	            .addSchemaArgs(field.args)
126	            .createExport(),
127	        )
128	      }
129	    }
130	
131	    for (const field of this.type.fields) {
132	      if (!field.args.length) {
133	        continue
134	      }
135	      const fieldOutput = this.dmmf.resolveOutputObjectType(field.outputType)
136	      if (!fieldOutput) {
137	        continue
138	      }
139	      argsTypes.push(
140	        new ArgsTypeBuilder(fieldOutput, this.context)
141	          .addSelectArg()
142	          .addOmitArg()
143	          .addIncludeArgIfHasRelations()
144	          .addSchemaArgs(field.args)
145	          .setGeneratedName(getModelFieldArgsName(field, this.model.name))
146	          .setComment(`${this.model.name}.${field.name}`)
147	          .createExport(),
148	      )
149	    }
150	
151	    argsTypes.push(
152	      new ArgsTypeBuilder(this.type, this.context)
153	        .addSelectArg()
154	        .addOmitArg()
155	        .addIncludeArgIfHasRelations()
156	        .createExport(),
157	    )
158	
159	    return argsTypes
160	  }
161	
162	  private rootFieldNameForAction(action: DMMF.ModelAction) {
163	    return this.mapping?.[action]
164	  }
165	
166	  private getGroupByTypes() {
167	    const { model, mapping } = this
168	
169	    const groupByType = this.dmmf.outputTypeMap.prisma[getGroupByName(model.name)]
170	    if (!groupByType) {
171	      throw new Error(`Could not get group by type for model ${model.name}`)
172	    }
173	
174	    const groupByRootField = this.dmmf.rootFieldMap[mapping!.groupBy!]
175	    if (!groupByRootField) {
176	      throw new Error(`Could not find groupBy root field for model ${model.name}. Mapping: ${mapping?.groupBy}`)
177	    }
178	
179	    const groupByArgsName = getGroupByArgsName(model.name)
180	
181	    return `
182	
183	
184	export type ${groupByArgsName}<ExtArgs extends runtime.Types.Extensions.InternalArgs = runtime.Types.Extensions.DefaultArgs> = {
185	${indent(
186	  groupByRootField.args
187	    .map((arg) => {
188	      const updatedArg = { ...arg, comment: getArgFieldJSDoc(this.type, DMMF.ModelAction.groupBy, arg) }
189	      return new InputField(updatedArg, this.context).toTS()
190	    })
191	    .concat(
192	      groupByType.fields
193	        .filter((f) => f.outputType.location === 'outputObjectTypes')
194	        .map((f) => {
195	          if (f.outputType.location === 'outputObjectTypes') {
196	            return `${f.name}?: ${getAggregateInputType(f.outputType.type)}${f.name === '_count' ? ' | true' : ''}`
197	          }
198	
199	          // to make TS happy, but can't happen, as we filter for outputObjectTypes
200	          return ''
201	        }),
202	    )
203	    .join('\n'),
204	  TAB_SIZE,
205	)}
206	}
207	
208	${ts.stringify(buildOutputType(groupByType))}
209	
210	export type ${getGroupByPayloadName(model.name)}<T extends ${groupByArgsName}> = Prisma.PrismaPromise<
211	  Array<
212	    Prisma.PickEnumerable<${groupByType.name}, T['by']> &
213	      {
214	        [P in ((keyof T) & (keyof ${groupByType.name}))]: P extends '_count'
215	          ? T[P] extends boolean
216	            ? number
217	            : Prisma.GetScalarType<T[P], ${groupByType.name}[P]>
218	          : Prisma.GetScalarType<T[P], ${groupByType.name}[P]>
219	      }
220	    >
221	  >
222	`
223	  }
224	
225	  private getAggregationTypes() {
226	    const { model, mapping } = this
227	    let aggregateType = this.dmmf.outputTypeMap.prisma[getAggregateName(model.name)]
228	    if (!aggregateType) {
229	      throw new Error(`Could not get aggregate type "${getAggregateName(model.name)}" for "${model.name}"`)
230	    }
231	    aggregateType = klona(aggregateType)
232	
233	    const aggregateRootField = this.dmmf.rootFieldMap[mapping!.aggregate!]
234	    if (!aggregateRootField) {
235	      throw new Error(`Could not find aggregate root field for model ${model.name}. Mapping: ${mapping?.aggregate}`)
236	    }
237	
238	    const aggregateTypes = [aggregateType]
239	
240	    const avgType = this.dmmf.outputTypeMap.prisma[getAvgAggregateName(model.name)]
241	    const sumType = this.dmmf.outputTypeMap.prisma[getSumAggregateName(model.name)]
242	    const minType = this.dmmf.outputTypeMap.prisma[getMinAggregateName(model.name)]
243	    const maxType = this.dmmf.outputTypeMap.prisma[getMaxAggregateName(model.name)]
244	    const countType = this.dmmf.outputTypeMap.prisma[getCountAggregateOutputName(model.name)]
245	
246	    if (avgType) {
247	      aggregateTypes.push(avgType)
248	    }
249	    if (sumType) {
250	      aggregateTypes.push(sumType)
251	    }
252	    if (minType) {
253	      aggregateTypes.push(minType)
254	    }
255	    if (maxType) {
256	      aggregateTypes.push(maxType)
257	    }
258	    if (countType) {
259	      aggregateTypes.push(countType)
260	    }
261	
262	    const aggregateArgsName = getAggregateArgsName(model.name)
263	
264	    const aggregateName = getAggregateName(model.name)
265	
266	    return `${aggregateTypes
267	      .map(buildOutputType)
268	      .map((type) => ts.stringify(type))
269	      .join('\n\n')}
270	
271	${
272	  aggregateTypes.length > 1
273	    ? aggregateTypes
274	        .slice(1)
275	        .map((type) => {
276	          const newType: DMMF.InputType = {
277	            name: getAggregateInputType(type.name),
278	            constraints: {
279	              maxNumFields: null,
280	              minNumFields: null,
281	            },
282	            fields: type.fields.map((field) => ({
283	              ...field,
284	              name: field.name,
285	              isNullable: false,
286	              isRequired: false,
287	              isParameterizable: false,
288	              inputTypes: [
289	                {
290	                  isList: false,
291	                  location: 'scalar',
292	                  type: 'true',
293	                },
294	              ],
295	            })),
296	          }
297	          return new InputType(newType, this.context).toTS()
298	        })
299	        .join('\n')
300	    : ''
301	}
302	
303	export type ${aggregateArgsName}<ExtArgs extends runtime.Types.Extensions.InternalArgs = runtime.Types.Extensions.DefaultArgs> = {
304	${indent(
305	  aggregateRootField.args
306	    .map((arg) => {
307	      const updatedArg = { ...arg, comment: getArgFieldJSDoc(this.type, DMMF.ModelAction.aggregate, arg) }
308	      return new InputField(updatedArg, this.context).toTS()
309	    })
310	    .concat(
311	      aggregateType.fields.map((f) => {
312	        let data = ''
313	        const comment = getArgFieldJSDoc(this.type, DMMF.ModelAction.aggregate, f.name)
314	        data += comment ? wrapComment(comment) + '\n' : ''
315	        if (f.name === '_count' || f.name === 'count') {
316	          data += `${f.name}?: true | ${getCountAggregateInputName(model.name)}`
317	        } else {
318	          data += `${f.name}?: ${getAggregateInputType(f.outputType.type)}`
319	        }
320	        return data
321	      }),
322	    )
323	    .join('\n'),
324	  TAB_SIZE,
325	)}
326	}
327	
328	export type ${getAggregateGetName(model.name)}<T extends ${getAggregateArgsName(model.name)}> = {
329	      [P in keyof T & keyof ${aggregateName}]: P extends '_count' | 'count'
330	    ? T[P] extends true
331	      ? number
332	      : Prisma.GetScalarType<T[P], ${aggregateName}[P]>
333	    : Prisma.GetScalarType<T[P], ${aggregateName}[P]>
334	}`
335	  }
336	
337	  private getDeepInputTypes() {
338	    return this.dmmf.inputObjectTypes.prisma
339	      ?.filter((i) => i.meta?.grouping === this.model.name)
340	      .map((inputType) => new InputType(inputType, this.context).toTS())
341	      .join('\n')
342	  }
343	
344	  private getCountTypes() {
345	    const countTypes: Count[] = this.dmmf.schema.outputObjectTypes.prisma
346	      ?.filter((t) => t.name === getCountOutputTypeName(this.model.name))
347	      .map((t) => new Count(t, this.context))
348	
349	    return countTypes.map((t) => t.toTS()).join('\n')
350	  }
351	
352	  private getModelExport(): string {
353	    const docLines = this.model.documentation ?? ''
354	    const modelLine = `Model ${this.model.name}\n`
355	    const docs = `${modelLine}${docLines}`
356	
357	    const modelTypeExport = ts
358	      .moduleExport(
359	        ts.typeDeclaration(
360	          `${this.model.name}Model`,
361	          ts
362	            .namedType(`runtime.Types.Result.DefaultSelection`)
363	            .addGenericArgument(ts.namedType(getPayloadName(this.model.name))),
364	        ),
365	      )
366	      .setDocComment(ts.docComment(docs))
367	
368	    return ts.stringify(modelTypeExport)
369	  }
370	
371	  public toTS(): string {
372	    const { model } = this
373	    const isComposite = this.dmmf.isComposite(model.name)
374	
375	    const omitType = ts.stringify(
376	      buildOmitType({ modelName: this.model.name, context: this.context, fields: this.type.fields }),
377	      {
378	        newLine: 'leading',
379	      },
380	    )
381	
382	    const hasRelationField = model.fields.some((f) => f.kind === 'object')
383	    const includeType = hasRelationField
384	      ? ts.stringify(
385	          buildIncludeType({ modelName: this.model.name, context: this.context, fields: this.type.fields }),
386	          {
387	            newLine: 'leading',
388	          },
389	        )
390	      : ''
391	
392	    const createManyAndReturnIncludeType =
393	      hasRelationField && this.createManyAndReturnType
394	        ? ts.stringify(
395	            buildIncludeType({
396	              typeName: getIncludeCreateManyAndReturnName(this.model.name),
397	              modelName: this.model.name,
398	              context: this.context,
399	              fields: this.createManyAndReturnType.fields,
400	            }),

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document.

## Summary: DMMF Types in Prisma Generators

Based on my exploration of the Prisma codebase, here's a comprehensive guide to the DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) types used in generators:

### 1. **DMMF Type Definitions**

The DMMF types are defined in `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`. Here are the key structures:

#### **DMMF.Document**
The root structure passed to generators via `options.dmmf`:

```typescript
export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Datamodel**
Contains the actual data model from your Prisma schema:

```typescript
export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]  // Composite types
  indexes: Index[]
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Model**
Represents a model or composite type definition:

```typescript
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Field**
Represents a single field within a model:

```typescript
export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind  // 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean
  type: string  // BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null  // e.g., ['VarChar', ['191']]
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]  // For relations
  relationToFields?: string[]    // For relations
  relationOnDelete?: string      // e.g., 'Cascade'
  relationOnUpdate?: string      // e.g., 'Cascade'
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>
```

#### **DMMF.Mappings**
Maps models to their GraphQL operations:

```typescript
export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>
```

#### **DMMF.ModelMapping**
Maps a model to its available operations:

```typescript
export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>
```

### 2. **Accessing DMMF in Generators**

When your `onGenerate` handler is called, it receives `GeneratorOptions`:

**File:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`

```typescript
export type GeneratorOptions = {
  generator: GeneratorConfig
  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
  schemaPath: string
  dmmf: DMMF.Document              // This is what you need
  datasources: DataSource[]
  datamodel: string                 // Raw Prisma schema string
  version: string
  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
  noHints?: boolean
  allowNoModels?: boolean
  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
}
```

### 3. **Usage Patterns**

#### **Iterating Over Models**

```typescript
// Access all models from the DMMF
const models = options.dmmf.datamodel.models

// Iterate over each model
for (const model of models) {
  console.log(model.name)  // e.g., "User", "Post"
  
  // Iterate over fields
  for (const field of model.fields) {
    console.log(field.name)      // e.g., "id", "email"
    console.log(field.type)      // e.g., "String", "Int", "User"
    console.log(field.kind)      // e.g., "scalar", "object", "enum"
    console.log(field.isRequired) // boolean
    console.log(field.isList)    // boolean
  }
}
```

#### **Accessing Enums**

```typescript
const enums = options.dmmf.datamodel.enums

for (const enumType of enums) {
  console.log(enumType.name)    // e.g., "Role"
  for (const value of enumType.values) {
    console.log(value.name)     // e.g., "ADMIN", "USER"
    console.log(value.dbName)   // Database name, if mapped
  }
}
```

#### **Checking Field Kind**

```typescript
const field = model.fields[0]

if (field.kind === 'scalar') {
  // Scalar field (String, Int, Boolean, etc.)
  console.log(`Scalar: ${field.name} of type ${field.type}`)
} else if (field.kind === 'object') {
  // Relation field
  console.log(`Relation: ${field.name} to ${field.type}`)
  console.log(`From fields: ${field.relationFromFields}`)
  console.log(`To fields: ${field.relationToFields}`)
} else if (field.kind === 'enum') {
  // Enum field
  console.log(`Enum: ${field.name} of type ${field.type}`)
}
```

#### **Accessing Model Operations**

```typescript
const mapping = options.dmmf.mappings.modelOperations.find(m => m.model === 'User')

if (mapping?.findUnique) {
  console.log('findUnique operation name:', mapping.findUnique)
}
if (mapping?.create) {
  console.log('create operation name:', mapping.create)
}
```

### 4. **Real-World Example from Prisma Client Generator**

**File:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/TSClient/TSClient.ts` (Lines 49-56)

```typescript
const modelNames = Object.values(context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap)
  .filter((model) => context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name])
  .map((model) => model.name)

const modelsFileMap: FileMap = modelNames.reduce((acc, modelName) => {
  acc[context.outputFileName(modelName)] = createModelFile(context, modelName)
  return acc
}, {})
```

### 5. **Helper Class: DMMFHelper**

Prisma generators use the `DMMFHelper` class for convenient access:

**File:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export class DMMFHelper implements DMMF.Document {
  constructor(public document: DMMF.Document) {}

  get datamodel(): DMMF.Datamodel {
    return this.document.datamodel
  }

  get mappings(): DMMF.Mappings {
    return this.document.mappings
  }

  get schema(): DMMF.Schema {
    return this.document.schema
  }

  get typeAndModelMap(): Dictionary<DMMF.Model> {
    // Combined map of all models and types
    return (this._typeAndModelMap ??= this.buildTypeModelMap())
  }

  get mappingsMap(): Dictionary<DMMF.ModelMapping> {
    // Map models to their operations
    return (this._mappingsMap ??= this.buildMappingsMap())
  }

  isComposite(modelOrTypeName: string) {
    return this.compositeNames.has(modelOrTypeName)
  }
}
```

### 6. **Key Type Enums**

```typescript
export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}
```

### Key File Locations

- **DMMF Type Definitions:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`
- **GeneratorOptions:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`
- **GeneratorHandler:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`
- **DMMFHelper:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/dmmf.ts`
- **Usage Examples:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/TSClient/`

This structure allows generators to inspect and process your Prisma schema at build time, generating type-safe client code and other artifacts.


### 4. **Real-World Example from Prisma Client Generator**

**File:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/TSClient/TSClient.ts` (Lines 49-56)

```typescript
const modelNames = Object.values(context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap)
  .filter((model) => context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name])
  .map((model) => model.name)

const modelsFileMap: FileMap = modelNames.reduce((acc, modelName) => {
  acc[context.outputFileName(modelName)] = createModelFile(context, modelName)
  return acc
}, {})
```

### 5. **Helper Class: DMMFHelper**

Prisma generators use the `DMMFHelper` class for convenient access:

**File:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/dmmf.ts`

```typescript
export class DMMFHelper implements DMMF.Document {
  constructor(public document: DMMF.Document) {}

  get datamodel(): DMMF.Datamodel {
    return this.document.datamodel
  }

  get mappings(): DMMF.Mappings {
    return this.document.mappings
  }

  get schema(): DMMF.Schema {
    return this.document.schema
  }

  get typeAndModelMap(): Dictionary<DMMF.Model> {
    // Combined map of all models and types
    return (this._typeAndModelMap ??= this.buildTypeModelMap())
  }

  get mappingsMap(): Dictionary<DMMF.ModelMapping> {
    // Map models to their operations
    return (this._mappingsMap ??= this.buildMappingsMap())
  }

  isComposite(modelOrTypeName: string) {
    return this.compositeNames.has(modelOrTypeName)
  }
}
```

### 6. **Key Type Enums**

```typescript
export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}
```

### Key File Locations

- **DMMF Type Definitions:** `/packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`
- **GeneratorOptions:** `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts`
- **GeneratorHandler:** `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`
- **DMMFHelper:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/dmmf.ts`
- **Usage Examples:** `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/TSClient/`

This structure allows generators to inspect and process your Prisma schema at build time, generating type-safe client code and other artifacts.

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
}>

export type FieldDefault = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: Array<string | number>
}>

export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number

export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  type: IndexType
  isDefinedOnField: boolean
  name?: string
  dbName?: string
  algorithm?: string
  clustered?: boolean
  fields: IndexField[]
}>

export type IndexType = 'id' | 'normal' | 'unique' | 'fulltext'

export type IndexField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  sortOrder?: SortOrder
  length?: number
  operatorClass?: string
}>

export type SortOrder = 'asc' | 'desc'

export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{
  rootQueryType?: string
  rootMutationType?: string
  inputObjectTypes: {
    // For now there are no `model` InputTypes
    model?: InputType[]
    prisma?: InputType[]
  }
  outputObjectTypes: {
    model: OutputType[]
    prisma: OutputType[]
  }
  enumTypes: {
    model?: SchemaEnum[]
    prisma: SchemaEnum[]
  }
  fieldRefTypes: {
    prisma?: FieldRefType[]
  }
}>

export type Query = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  args: SchemaArg[]
  output: QueryOutput
}>

export type QueryOutput = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
}>

export type TypeRef<AllowedLocations extends FieldLocation> = {
  isList: boolean
  type: string
  location: AllowedLocations
  namespace?: FieldNamespace
}

export type InputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'>

export type SchemaArg = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  comment?: string
  isNullable: boolean
  isRequired: boolean
  inputTypes: InputTypeRef[]
  isParameterizable: boolean
  requiresOtherFields?: string[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
}>

export type OutputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: SchemaField[]
}>

export type SchemaField = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  isNullable?: boolean
  outputType: OutputTypeRef
  args: SchemaArg[]
  deprecation?: Deprecation
  documentation?: string
}>

export type OutputTypeRef = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes'>

export type Deprecation = ReadonlyDeep<{
  sinceVersion: string
  reason: string
  plannedRemovalVersion?: string
}>

export type InputType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  constraints: {
    maxNumFields: number | null
    minNumFields: number | null
    fields?: string[]
  }
  meta?: {
    source?: string
    grouping?: string
  }
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefType = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  allowTypes: FieldRefAllowType[]
  fields: SchemaArg[]
}>

export type FieldRefAllowType = TypeRef<'scalar' | 'enumTypes'>

export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{
  model: string
  plural: string
  findUnique?: string | null
  findUniqueOrThrow?: string | null
  findFirst?: string | null
  findFirstOrThrow?: string | null
  findMany?: string | null
  create?: string | null
  createMany?: string | null
  createManyAndReturn?: string | null
  update?: string | null
  updateMany?: string | null
  updateManyAndReturn?: string | null
  upsert?: string | null
  delete?: string | null
  deleteMany?: string | null
  aggregate?: string | null
  groupBy?: string | null
  count?: string | null
  findRaw?: string | null
  aggregateRaw?: string | null
}>

export enum ModelAction {
  findUnique = 'findUnique',
  findUniqueOrThrow = 'findUniqueOrThrow',
  findFirst = 'findFirst',
  findFirstOrThrow = 'findFirstOrThrow',
  findMany = 'findMany',
  create = 'create',
  createMany = 'createMany',
  createManyAndReturn = 'createManyAndReturn',
  update = 'update',
  updateMany = 'updateMany',
  updateManyAndReturn = 'updateManyAndReturn',
  upsert = 'upsert',
  delete = 'delete',
  deleteMany = 'deleteMany',
  groupBy = 'groupBy',
  count = 'count', // TODO: count does not actually exist in DMMF
  aggregate = 'aggregate',
  findRaw = 'findRaw',
  aggregateRaw = 'aggregateRaw',
}

input.prompt:
Design a detailed implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package at `packages/generator-rest-client/` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Background Context

### Generator Protocol
Generators in Prisma use a JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol over stdin/stderr. The entry point is `generatorHandler()` from `@prisma/generator-helper` which accepts a `Handler` with:
- `onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest` - returns metadata
- `onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>` - main generation logic

`GeneratorOptions` includes:
- `generator: GeneratorConfig` (with `output` as `EnvValue`)
- `dmmf: DMMF.Document` containing `datamodel.models`, `datamodel.enums`, `mappings`
- `datasources`, `datamodel` (raw schema string), etc.

Key DMMF types (from `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`):
- `Model`: `{ name, fields: Field[], primaryKey, uniqueFields, documentation }`
- `Field`: `{ kind: 'scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported', name, type: string, isRequired, isList, isId, isUnique, isReadOnly, hasDefaultValue, relationFromFields?, relationToFields?, relationName? }`
- `DatamodelEnum`: `{ name, values: EnumValue[] }`
- Scalar types: `String`, `Int`, `BigInt`, `Float`, `Boolean`, `DateTime`, `Json`, `Bytes`, `Decimal`

### Package Structure Conventions
Based on existing packages like `credentials-store`, `dmmf`, `adapter-pg`:

**package.json pattern:**
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/<name>",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": { ".": { "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" }, "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" } } },
  "repository": { "type": "git", "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git", "directory": "packages/<name>" },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "scripts": { "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts", "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts", "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build", "test": "vitest run" },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

**tsconfig.json:** `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }`
**tsconfig.build.json:** `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src"] }`
**helpers/build.ts:** Uses `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts` for bundled CJS+ESM output.

### Existing Generator Implementations
- `packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`: Implements `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator` with `getManifest()` and `generate()` methods. Uses `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` to get output dir.
- `packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`: Similar pattern.
- Both are complex — they generate full Prisma Client runtimes. Our generator is much simpler.

### The generatorHandler function (from `@prisma/generator-helper`)
```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}
export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void { /* JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr */ }
```

This is the simpler API for external-style generators. It's the right approach for our package since it's simpler than implementing the `Generator` interface used by the built-in generators.

### What the generator should produce
For a schema like:
```prisma
model User {
  id    Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  email String  @unique
  name  String?
  posts Post[]
}
model Post {
  id        Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  title     String
  content   String?
  published Boolean @default(false)
  author    User    @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
  authorId  Int
}
```

Generate a `rest-client.ts` file containing:
1. TypeScript interfaces for each model (scalar fields only, no relation objects)
2. CRUD methods per model: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
3. A `RestClient` class that takes a `baseUrl` and provides typed methods

### Requirements
1. `src/index.ts` - Entry point using `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`
2. `src/generator.ts` - Main generation logic parsing DMMF and producing code
3. `src/template.ts` - String template helpers for code generation
4. Output: single `rest-client.ts` file to the configured output directory
5. Follow all monorepo conventions (kebab-case filenames, no barrel files unless needed, Apache-2.0)

### Design Questions to Address
1. How to map Prisma scalar types to TypeScript types
2. How to handle enum types in generated interfaces
3. How to identify the unique/ID field for `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` (need an identifier)
4. Whether to include relation fields in the interface (probably not for REST - just the scalar FK fields)
5. How to structure the generated `RestClient` class
6. How to handle the `bin` entry for the generator (since Prisma runs generators as child processes via `provider` field in schema)

Please produce a detailed implementation plan covering:
- File-by-file breakdown of what to create
- Exact dependencies needed
- The generated output format (show example generated code)
- Type mapping strategy
- How the generator binary/entry point works (bin field or direct tsx execution)
- Test strategy

Design a detailed implementation plan for a new Prisma generator package at `packages/generator-rest-client/` that generates a typed REST API client from a Prisma schema.

## Background Context

### Generator Protocol
Generators in Prisma use a JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol over stdin/stderr. The entry point is `generatorHandler()` from `@prisma/generator-helper` which accepts a `Handler` with:
- `onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest` - returns metadata
- `onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>` - main generation logic

`GeneratorOptions` includes:
- `generator: GeneratorConfig` (with `output` as `EnvValue`)
- `dmmf: DMMF.Document` containing `datamodel.models`, `datamodel.enums`, `mappings`
- `datasources`, `datamodel` (raw schema string), etc.

Key DMMF types (from `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts`):
- `Model`: `{ name, fields: Field[], primaryKey, uniqueFields, documentation }`
- `Field`: `{ kind: 'scalar'|'object'|'enum'|'unsupported', name, type: string, isRequired, isList, isId, isUnique, isReadOnly, hasDefaultValue, relationFromFields?, relationToFields?, relationName? }`
- `DatamodelEnum`: `{ name, values: EnumValue[] }`
- Scalar types: `String`, `Int`, `BigInt`, `Float`, `Boolean`, `DateTime`, `Json`, `Bytes`, `Decimal`

### Package Structure Conventions
Based on existing packages like `credentials-store`, `dmmf`, `adapter-pg`:

**package.json pattern:**
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/<name>",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": { ".": { "require": { "types": "./dist/index.d.ts", "default": "./dist/index.js" }, "import": { "types": "./dist/index.d.mts", "default": "./dist/index.mjs" } } },
  "repository": { "type": "git", "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git", "directory": "packages/<name>" },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "scripts": { "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts", "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts", "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build", "test": "vitest run" },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

**tsconfig.json:** `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.json", "include": ["./**/*"] }`
**tsconfig.build.json:** `{ "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json", "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "dist", "rootDir": "src" }, "include": ["src"] }`
**helpers/build.ts:** Uses `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts` for bundled CJS+ESM output.

### Existing Generator Implementations
- `packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`: Implements `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator` with `getManifest()` and `generate()` methods. Uses `parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)` to get output dir.
- `packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`: Similar pattern.
- Both are complex — they generate full Prisma Client runtimes. Our generator is much simpler.

### The generatorHandler function (from `@prisma/generator-helper`)
```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>

}
export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void { /* JSON-RPC over stdin/stderr */ }
```

This is the simpler API for external-style generators. It's the right approach for our package since it's simpler than implementing the `Generator` interface used by the built-in generators.

### What the generator should produce
For a schema like:
```prisma
model User {
  id    Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  email String  @unique
  name  String?
  posts Post[]
}
model Post {
  id        Int     @id @default(autoincrement())
  title     String
  content   String?
  published Boolean @default(false)
  author    User    @relation(fields: [authorId], references: [id])
  authorId  Int
}
```

Generate a `rest-client.ts` file containing:
1. TypeScript interfaces for each model (scalar fields only, no relation objects)
2. CRUD methods per model: `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
3. A `RestClient` class that takes a `baseUrl` and provides typed methods

### Requirements
1. `src/index.ts` - Entry point using `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`
2. `src/generator.ts` - Main generation logic parsing DMMF and producing code
3. `src/template.ts` - String template helpers for code generation
4. Output: single `rest-client.ts` file to the configured output directory
5. Follow all monorepo conventions (kebab-case filenames, no barrel files unless needed, Apache-2.0)

### Design Questions to Address
1. How to map Prisma scalar types to TypeScript types
2. How to handle enum types in generated interfaces
3. How to identify the unique/ID field for `findUnique`/`update`/`delete` (need an identifier)

78	  isReadOnly: boolean

84	   */
85	  type: string
86	  /**

89	   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.

91	  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
92	  dbName?: string | null
93	  hasDefaultValue: boolean

95	  relationFromFields?: string[]
96	  relationToFields?: string[]
97	  relationOnDelete?: string
98	  relationOnUpdate?: string
99	  relationName?: string
100	  documentation?: string

102

106	}>
107	
108	export type FieldDefaultScalar = string | boolean | number
109	
110	export type Index = ReadonlyDeep<{

115	  dbName?: string
116	  algorithm?: string
117	  clustered?: boolean

122

131	
132	export type Schema = ReadonlyDeep<{

220

226

228	
229	export type ModelMapping = ReadonlyDeep<{

22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}

5	 * Parses an EnvValue (example: Provider) and return the string value

9	 * - If there is an env var is present but can't be resolved an error will be thrown
10	 */
11	export function parseEnvValue(object: EnvValue) {
12	  if (object.fromEnvVar && object.fromEnvVar != 'null') {
13	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]
14	    if (!value) {
15	      throw new Error(
16	        `Attempted to load provider value using \`env(${
17	          object.fromEnvVar
18	        })\` but it was not present. Please ensure that ${dim(
19	          object.fromEnvVar,
20	        )} is present in your Environment Variables`,
21	      )
22	    }
23	
24	    return value
25	  }
26	
27	  return object.value as string
28	}

33	 * - If there is no env var just return the value
34	 * - If there is an env var it will be resolve and returned.
35	 * - If there is an env var is present but can't be resolved an error will be thrown
36	 */
37	export function parseBinaryTargetsEnvValue(object: BinaryTargetsEnvValue): string[] | string {
38	  if (object.fromEnvVar && object.fromEnvVar != 'null') {
39	    const value = process.env[object.fromEnvVar]
40	    if (!value) {
41	      throw new Error(
42	        `Attempted to load binaryTargets value using \`env(${
43	          object.fromEnvVar
44	        })\` but it was not present. Please ensure that ${dim(
45	          object.fromEnvVar,
46	        )} is present in your Environment Variables`,
47	      )

49	
50	    // value is a string because it's from env var but need to be parsed
51	    // can be an array like `["darwin"]`
52	    // or a string like `"darwin"`
53	
54	    return JSON.parse(value)
55	  }
56	
57	  return object.value as any
